replication_study.hypothesis: There is an interaction between participant culture (European American vs East Asian) and writing condition (mortality-salience vs control) such that PASAverage (mean of the Prostitution Attitudes Scale items) changes in opposite directions across cultures: mortality salience increases PASAverage for East Asian participants but decreases PASAverage for European American participants. Formally: H0: no Culture × WritingCondition interaction on PASAverage; H1: significant Culture × WritingCondition interaction on PASAverage in the predicted (divergent) direction.
replication_study.study_type: Experimental (between-subjects online experiment sample used to replicate original laboratory experiment)

data_plan.dataset_identifier: Ma-Kellams Replication Study Data.sav (version provided in data/original/33/input/replication_data)
data_plan.source_type: Online experiment survey dataset (.sav SPSS file) containing responses to an experimental writing manipulation, PANAS, PAS items, bail judgments, and demographics.
data_plan.wave_or_subset: Full provided dataset (all cases) with planned analytic subsets matching SPSS script filters (e.g., by Culture and WritingCondition; also subsets excluding low word counts or Prolific flag if required).
data_plan.sample_size: Total rows in dataset: 477. PASAverage non-missing: 474 (see dataset info). Effective sample for primary ANOVA depends on complete cases for PASAverage, Culture, and WritingCondition (approximately 474).
data_plan.unit_of_analysis: Individual participants (each survey response is one unit).
data_plan.access_details: Dataset provided in repository at data/original/33/input/replication_data/Ma-Kellams Replication Study Data.sav. For external replication, data access process is not specified in original materials (post_registration.json notes 'not stated').

qualification.explanation: This dataset is qualified because it contains the operational measures required to test the focal claim: PAS items (PAS1..PAS5), experimental writing manipulation indicator (WritingCondition), culture/ethnicity indicators (CulturalBackground, EuropeanAmerican, EastAsian, and derived Culture), and PANAS items for mood control. The SPSS syntax provided in the replication_data folder implements the same scoring and filters used to compute PASAverage, PANASNetScore, and to run the 2 (Culture) × 2 (WritingCondition) ANOVAs, making this dataset high-fidelity for reproducing the original test.
qualification.similarity_to_original: Matches original study constructs and analysis approach: original paper tested 2 (condition: mortality salience vs control) × 2 (ethnicity: European American vs Asian American) ANOVAs on Prostitution Attitudes Scale scores (initial_details.txt and post_registration.json). The replication dataset includes the same PAS items, a WritingCondition variable representing the mortality-salience vs control manipulation, and culture/ethnicity coding. Evidence: initial_details.txt (CLAIM and HYPOTHESIS) and post_registration.json (method/outcome/independent variables). The SPSS syntax (data/original/33/input/replication_data/Ma-Kellams Replication Analysis Syntax.sps) shows explicit recoding and computation of PASAverage, PANASNetScore, Culture, and the exact ANOVA commands used to test the interaction.
qualification.deviation_from_original: Deviates from the original lab samples in collection mode and sample composition: the original studies were laboratory experiments with small college-student samples (Study 1 N=42; Study 2 N=71) as described in post_registration.json (original_study.data). The provided replication dataset is larger (N=477) and appears to include online-sourced participants (the SPSS syntax includes a Prolific variable and filters testing Prolific vs non-Prolific), indicating a different recruitment mode. Specific references: post_registration.json (original sample size and lab recruitment) vs. replication_data files (SPSS syntax data/original/33/input/replication_data/Ma-Kellams Replication Analysis Syntax.sps and dataset data/original/33/input/replication_data/Ma-Kellams Replication Study Data.sav). Thus this dataset provides a new sample collected under related experimental conditions but not identical to the original lab sample — appropriate for testing robustness.
qualification.notes: Caveats and data specifics: (1) Some variables have missingness (PASAverage non-missing=474; Culture variable has some missing values per dataset info). (2) The SPSS syntax recodes PAS2 and PAS5 as reversed items (PAS2Positive, PAS5Positive) before averaging; replication code must implement the same recoding (this is implemented in the provided Python script). (3) The dataset includes additional filters and robustness checks in the SPSS syntax (e.g., excluding low word-count respondents, excluding outliers on PASAverage or BailAmount, Prolific subsamples) that can be applied; these are described in the syntax file. (4) Ensure all file IO in scripts uses the /app/data mount (the provided Python script reads from /app/data/replication_data and writes to /app/data/replication_data). Files in the repository are at data/original/33/input/replication_data; execution environment should mount these to /app/data/replication_data when running analysis. (5) Variable coding notes and recoding logic are in SPSS syntax: data/original/33/input/replication_data/Ma-Kellams Replication Analysis Syntax.sps.

steps: 1) Load the SPSS dataset into Python (pyreadstat) from /app/data/replication_data/Ma-Kellams Replication Study Data.sav.

2) Recode variables according to the SPSS syntax: create Wrote30WordsOrMore, EuropeanAmerican/EastAsian, Culture (0=EuropeanAmerican, 1=EastAsian), USBorn/EastAsianBorn, reverse-scored PAS2 and PAS5 (PAS2Positive, PAS5Positive), compute PASAverage = mean(PAS1, PAS2Positive, PAS3, PAS4, PAS5Positive), compute PANASNetScore (sum of positive PANAS items minus sum of negative items), and create BailAmount transforms (sqrt, log) as in the syntax file.
3) Run primary test: 2 (Culture: 0 vs 1) × 2 (WritingCondition) ANOVA on PASAverage (Type III sums of squares).
4) Report ANOVA table (F, df, p, partial eta-squared), group means and standard errors for PASAverage by Culture × WritingCondition, and direction of effects.

5) Follow-up planned contrasts: (a) within-culture t-tests comparing WritingCondition groups (mortality vs control) for European Americans and East Asians; (b) tests of simple effects and comparisons reported in original paper.
6) Fit ANCOVA including PANASNetScore as covariate to evaluate mood confounding (per SPSS syntax).

7) Run robustness checks per SPSS syntax: exclude participants with WordCount <= thresholds (e.g., <30 words, <1 word), exclude outliers on PASAverage, restrict sample to EuropeanAmerican or EastAsianBorn where indicated, test subsamples by Prolific flag, run BailAmount analyses and transformations.
8) Save outputs and summary results to /app/data/replication_data/replication_results.txt and any additional CSVs needed for reporting.
planned_method.models: Ordinary Least Squares (OLS) linear models for ANOVA (equivalent to UNIANOVA in SPSS) with categorical main effects and interaction (implemented via Patsy formulas and statsmodels). Type III sum-of-squares ANOVA tables via statsmodels.api.stats.anova_lm. Two-sample t-tests (Welch) for follow-ups and planned contrasts.
planned_method.outcome_variable: PASAverage (mean of PAS items after reversing PAS2 and PAS5 as in SPSS syntax).
planned_method.independent_variables: Primary: Culture (derived: 0=European American, 1=East Asian), WritingCondition (0=control, 1=mortality-salience). Interaction term Culture × WritingCondition.
planned_method.control_variables: PANASNetScore (mood covariate) will be included in supplemental ANCOVA models; demographic checks for gender, age, Prolific membership can be run as additional controls but were non-significant in original study.
planned_method.tools_software: Python 3.10+; packages: pandas, numpy, pyreadstat, statsmodels, scipy. Scripts execute in a container or environment where /app/data is mounted to include the replication_data files.

planned_estimation_and_test.estimation: Estimate coefficients for main effects and the Culture × WritingCondition interaction in an OLS model for PASAverage. Report F-statistic for the interaction (Type III), partial eta-squared (if computed), group means and standard errors, and t-statistics for follow-up contrasts.
planned_estimation_and_test.test: Primary test: F-test for the Culture × WritingCondition interaction (two-sided, α=0.05). Follow-ups: Welch two-sample t-tests for within-culture writing condition comparisons; ANCOVA F-tests for the same interaction controlling for PANASNetScore.

planned_method.missing_data_handling: Primary plan: listwise deletion for cases missing any variables required by a given analysis (PASAverage and the factors in that analysis). Report N for each analysis. For robustness, consider multiple imputation for missing covariates only if missingness is substantial and ignorable, but primary replication will match SPSS approach (listwise deletion per ANOVA requirements).
planned_method.multiple_testing_policy: Primary confirmatory test is the Culture × WritingCondition interaction on PASAverage. Secondary analyses (within-culture t-tests, bail analyses) are treated as exploratory; if multiple confirmatory hypotheses beyond the primary interaction are pre-specified, apply Bonferroni correction. For transparency, report raw p-values and adjusted p-values where appropriate.
planned_method.inference_criteria: Declare replication of the focal claim if the Culture × WritingCondition interaction is significant at α=0.05 (two-tailed) and the pattern of means matches the original directional prediction (European Americans show decreased PASAverage under mortality salience vs control; East Asian participants show increased PASAverage). Also report effect sizes and confidence intervals to assess magnitude and practical significance.

files.Ma-Kellams_Replication_Analysis__py.py: Python script that reproduces the SPSS computations and runs the primary tests. It performs: reading the .sav file using pyreadstat, variable recoding (reverse scoring PAS2 and PAS5, computing PASAverage and PANASNetScore, deriving Culture), running the 2x2 ANOVA (OLS with interaction) and Type III ANOVA table, follow-up t-tests for within-culture contrasts, ANCOVA with PANASNetScore, and robustness checks (exclude participants with low word counts). The script writes a summary text output to /app/data/replication_data/replication_results.txt. It is designed as the execution entrypoint for the replication.
files.Ma-Kellams Replication Study Data.sav: Input dataset (SPSS .sav) containing participant responses. The Python script reads this file and computes the PASAverage and other derived variables. This file is treated as input and is not modified.
files.Ma-Kellams Replication Analysis Syntax.s: Reference SPSS syntax used to derive variables and tests. Not executed directly in the replication pipeline, but used as a blueprint for recoding and analysis logic to ensure fidelity to the original calculations. The Python script implements the same logic and filters.

codebase.notes: Design notes and execution environment: The main executable is Ma-Kellams_Replication_Analysis__py.py and expects the SPSS file to be available at /app/data/replication_data/Ma-Kellams Replication Study Data.sav; it writes outputs to /app/data/replication_data/replication_results.txt. Required Python packages include pyreadstat to read .sav files. The SPSS syntax is preserved as a reference; all analysis logic is translated to Python per the run policy. Ensure /app/data is mounted to the directory containing the replication_data files prior to execution. The script is deterministic; no random seeds required for the reported analyses.

analysis.instructions: 1) Ensure data is mounted so that /app/data/replication_data/Ma-Kellams Replication Study Data.sav is accessible. 2) Create a Python environment with required packages (see docker_specs). 3) Run: python /app/data/replication_data/Ma-Kellams_Replication_Analysis__py.py. 4) Inspect /app/data/replication_data/replication_results.txt for ANOVA tables, group means, t-tests, and additional robustness outputs. 5) If requested, export group means and model objects for further reporting (script can be extended to save CSVs).
analysis.comparison_metrics: Compare key statistics to original paper: (a) F-statistic and p-value for the Culture × WritingCondition interaction on PASAverage (main replication metric); (b) group means (PASAverage) and standard errors for the four Culture × WritingCondition cells; (c) t-statistics and p-values for the within-culture contrasts reported in the original (European American mortality vs control; East Asian mortality vs control); (d) effect size estimates (e.g., eta-squared or partial eta-squared and Cohen's d for contrasts) and their confidence intervals. Evaluate whether direction and statistical significance match the original claim (original results: interaction F(1,38)=10.263, p=.003 for PAS in Study 1, reported in post_registration.json and initial_details.txt).
